---
title: "00 · Kiselev — Geometry, Book I (Planimetry)"
subtitle: "Lines, triangles, parallels, quadrilaterals, circles, similarity, regular polygons, areas — the classical Russian Planimetry."
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **Kiselev *Planimetry*** track — Book I of *Геометрия*,
arguably the cleanest school presentation of plane Euclidean geometry
ever written. Givental's English translation has been used in
US classrooms since 2006 and remains the touchstone for proof-first
school geometry.

By the end of Planimetry you can:

- Reason from **definitions and axioms** to theorems with the rigour of
  Euclid, in the streamlined Kiselev arrangement.
- Prove the **triangle congruence criteria** (SSS, SAS, ASA) and apply
  them confidently in problems.
- Reason about **perpendiculars, slants, and parallels** and the angle
  sums those produce.
- Work with **quadrilaterals** — parallelograms, trapezoids,
  rectangles, rhombi, squares — as a hierarchy of properties.
- Prove the **inscribed angle theorem** and the chord/tangent
  relationships in a **circle**.
- Apply **locus** reasoning to constructions and proofs.
- Reason via **similarity** — the most powerful tool in school
  geometry.
- Compute **areas** and prove the formulas, including the
  area-of-a-circle limit.
- Work with **regular polygons** and the **circumference of a
  circle** ($\pi$ from below).

## Source

Givental's English translation (free, authoritative):
[Kiselev's Geometry, Book I — Planimetry, Sumizdat](https://www.cimat.mx/ciencia_para_jovenes/bachillerato/libros/kiselev/%5Bkiselev%5Dbook1_planimetry.pdf).
The PDF stays external; the pointers in each lesson cite Givental's
chapter and section numbers.

::: {.callout-warning}
**Citation, not transcription.** Givental's translation is
copyrighted. Each lesson **adapts** Kiselev's exposition and exercises
in its own words and points the learner at the source PDF for the
canonical proofs. Do not copy Givental's prose directly.
:::

## Lessons

The full Planimetry track, in book order:

1. [**01 · Lines and Angles (Ch 1)**](01-lines-and-angles/index.qmd) —
   segments, rays, angles, vertical-angles theorem.
2. [**02 · Triangle Congruence (Ch 2)**](02-triangle-congruence/index.qmd) —
   SSS, SAS, ASA, the isosceles theorem, exterior-angle inequality.
3. [**03 · Perpendiculars and Obliques (Ch 3)**](03-perpendiculars/index.qmd) —
   the unique perpendicular as shortest segment; HL congruence;
   perpendicular-bisector locus.
4. [**04 · Parallels (Ch 4)**](04-parallels/index.qmd) — the
   parallel-line postulate, alternate-interior angles, and the
   triangle-angle-sum proof.
5. [**05 · Quadrilaterals (Ch 5)**](05-quadrilaterals/index.qmd) —
   parallelograms, rectangles, rhombi, squares, trapezoids;
   midsegment / midline theorem.
6. [**06 · Circles (Ch 6)**](06-circles/index.qmd) — chord–arc
   relations, tangent-radius perpendicularity, inscribed-angle
   theorem, cyclic quadrilateral.
7. [**07 · Similarity (Ch 7)**](07-similarity/index.qmd) —
   Thales' intercepts, AA / SSS / SAS similarity, Pythagoras via
   similarity, angle-bisector theorem.
8. [**08 · Regular Polygons and Circumference (Ch 8)**](08-regular-polygons/index.qmd) —
   inscribed/circumscribed circles of a regular $n$-gon; the limit
   definition of $\pi$.
9. [**09 · Areas (Ch 9)**](09-areas/index.qmd) — parallelogram,
   triangle, trapezoid, regular $n$-gon, circle. Heron's formula.

**Track complete.** Ready for *Stereometry* (kg2).

## Sister synthesis tiers

The synthesis lessons covering the same ground sit in
[Tier 1 Foundations](../tier-1-foundations/index.qmd) (shapes and
angles, area), [Tier 2 Pre-Algebra](../tier-2-prealgebra/index.qmd)
(Pythagoras, similarity), and the CBSE
[Class 9](../tier-cbse-09-class-9/index.qmd) track (Euclid's geometry,
lines and angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, circles).
